title: Remove Fewest Intervals to Avoid Overlaps
description:
Given a set of closed intervals, remove the minimum number of intervals so that the rest are non-overlapping. Return the number of intervals removed.

    

Constraints:
    - 0 ≤ n ≤ 10^5
- -10^9 ≤ start ≤ end ≤ 10^9
explanation:
Intuition
This is the complement of selecting the maximum number of compatible intervals. Sort by end time and count how many intervals can be kept without overlap. The removals equal total minus kept.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Sort intervals by end ascending
2. Main Algorithm: Greedily keep an interval if its start ≥ last_end; update last_end to its end
3. Return Result: Return n - kept

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n log n) due to sorting
- Space Complexity: O(1) extra

Key Insights
- Earliest finishing keeps the most
- Closed intervals allow equality at endpoints

Common Pitfalls
- Mixing open/closed semantics changes the condition
- Sorting by start fails on counterexamples
hints: Count kept intervals, answer is total-kept
Equality at endpoints is non-overlap for closed intervals
